generation.image_prompt: Mystical 1900s lithography tarot card illustration. Clean bold black ink outlines. NO BORDERS. FULL BLEED IMAGE. WIDE FIELD OF VIEW. ANTHROPOMORPHIC: A Taiwan leopard cat standing upright on two legs, triumphantly riding a deer-like animal (sambar deer) through a cheering forest crowd. He holds a golden-crowned wand. Behind him, EXACTLY FIVE other wooden wands are held up by invisible or partially visible cats, making EXACTLY SIX wands total. All wands must be narrow and centered. Slender build, sharp facial structure, white ear spots, forehead stripes.
generation.narrative: A leopard cat that has managed to survive multiple road crossings and farm traps is celebrated as a survivor. Its victory represents the hard-won success of a species that refuses to vanish.

meanings.upright: Victory, public recognition, success after a hard journey, and being held up as a model of survival.
meanings.reversed: Falling from grace, lack of recognition, or the 'hollow' victory of a survivor whose habitat was still lost.

ecology.mapping_note: Six of Wands is the 'Conservation Success Story'—the individual that becomes the face of the movement and thrives despite all odds.

website.excerpt: A triumphant survivor, the leopard cat is celebrated for its resilience in the face of modern challenges.
website.seo_description: Six of Wands reimagined as leopard cat conservation success and victory.

meaning.zh: Victory, public recognition, success after a hard journey, and being held up as a model of survival.
meaning.en: Victory, public recognition, success after a hard journey, and being held up as a model of survival.
